reviews.text: I'm not a picky person, but this hotel was a joke.   First, the lobby is beautiful - you could stare at that ceiling all day and just soak in the history,  But unfortunately, that's where all of the positive ends.  The first room I was in was decent-sized, but the shower didn't drain and the mirror on the wall in the bathroom was broken.  In addition, there was supposed to be a tablet that you could use to review the in-room menu, but it didn't work.  After the first night, I asked to move to a different room given I was supposed to be there for 4 nights and the shower not draining was disgusting.  They told me they could move me into a room with a king-sized bed , which was technically an upgrade, but they would waive the fees.  Ok, sounds good.  Except the room that they moved me to was the size of a closet.  It was about 1/3 of the size of the room I had been in previously.   It was also dated, but at least it had a working shower (but the tablet still didn't work.  I won't be back.

reviews.title: Stop in to the lobby, look up at the gorgeous ceiling, stay somewhere with customer service.
reviews.negatives:
Some of our experiences during a 5 night stay: Registration has 12 booths, 2--sometimes 3--were staffed; each person took 2-4 minutes; 23 people in front of us; do the math. Customer Service via room phone was automated; we never heard back.  We asked for a room on a higher level with a view. We got the 12th floor with a view of the opposite wing's windows and shades. (Ok, not everyone can have a lake view, but there were rooms at our price range with street/city views, and we booked weeks ago.) Stairwells were dirty; elevators required waits. Fridge or microwave cost $40. each extra (per day or per visit I'm not sure). "We did away with coffee making appliances a while ago;" option is to leave the building or join the lonnnnnng line at the downstairs Starbucks. Loose mirror in bathroom. Hinges of cupboard broken in fitness room. Restroom in fitness room: disgraceful? yuk?? Both. No inroom recycling containers or notes how to place linens etc. if reusing. The room was big enough for a king bed with about 2' on one side, 5' on the other, 4' at the bottom. Part of the limited floor space taken up by the "convenience food and drink" minibar with a note stating that  lifting one of these items it will trigger a (highly inflated) charge of the item, plus a 22% convenience fee. Our request to have this minibar removed to give us 2'x2'x3' more room was denied.
There are alot of hotels in the loop; I know because I researched and opted for historical charm. Next trip I'll take my own advice: enjoy the lobby ceiling for a moment or two, after checking in to a hotel that values customers.

reviews.title: 5 days 4 night stay
reviews.text:
The hotel has great location, and the interior design looks great. However, the service is terrible.
To start off, I approached the reception counter upon arrival, and I informed the counter receptionist that I have checked in online, and the first thing she said was : “ then why are you here?” I then told her that I was asked from the app to approach the counter. She then proceeded to help, but constantly chatting with her colleague next to her while helping me to check in. When I asked her about breakfast, she told me breakfast is not included, but when I told her that I am sure that I booked with breakfast, then she checked again and finally saw it in the system, and proceeded to prepare the breakfast vouchers for me.  Not only am I supposed by how rude she was, I am also surprised at how incompetent she was. I had to ask for everything that I have booked, otherwise I would not have gotten what I paid for. It’s surprising that a hotel this grand, would have a service like that. 
And to make the matter worse, she forgot to take my credit card details, so the next day, I got calls around midday, to ask me to go to the reception again to run my credit card through, such a waste of my time. 
Furthermore, the next morning, a hotel staff came knocking at my door at 6.30am, and he would not stop knocking until I went to the door, and it turned he wanted to know if I ordered breakfast, and I told him I did not, and he just woke away without even apologizing for having woken me up so early in the morning. 
Then at the breakfast, when I ordered the smashed avocado with biscuits to go, I did not realize that they missed out the fruit bowl that was supposed to come with the order. I only realized that there was supposed to be a fruit bowl the next morning when I ordered the same thing again. 
Overall, the service from this hotel cannot get any worse, and I cannot imagine how this is supposed to be a four star Hilton  hotel  that charges this much per night.

reviews.title: I enjoyed my stay
reviews.text:
I recently had the opportunity of staying at the Palmer House Hilton Hotel during my visit to Chicago, and overall, it was a good experience.

One of the highlights of the Palmer House Hilton is its stunning lobby. As soon as I walked in, I was captivated by the grandeur and elegance of the space. The historical elements blended seamlessly with modern touches, creating an inviting and luxurious atmosphere.

The gym facilities at the hotel are top-notch. Equipped with state-of-the-art equipment. 
The hotel pool is decent but on the smaller side. While it may not offer the grandeur and spaciousness that the gym offers, it still manages to serve its purpose. The water is clean and well-maintained. The poolside area provides plenty of comfortable setting. However, if you're someone who enjoys swimming laps or engaging in water activities, the limited size may be a bit restrictive. Nevertheless, if you're simply looking to cool off or unwind after a long day, the pool adequately fulfills that need.

The hotel's location is undoubtedly one of its strongest selling points. Being within walking distance to Millennium Park, numerous restaurants, and a variety of stores, it made venturing out and discovering the vibrant city incredibly convenient. The proximity to these attractions truly added value to my stay at the Palmer House Hilton.

While my room at the hotel was comfortable, it was on the smaller side. The limited space could be a downside for those seeking a more spacious accommodation. Additionally, the queen-size bed felt more akin to a full-size bed, which could be a bit disappointing for guests expecting more room to spread out.

On the other hand, the room did feature ample closet space, allowing for convenient storage of personal belongings. This attention to detail was appreciated and added a practical touch to the overall room design.

In conclusion, the Palmer House Hilton Hotel is a charming and elegant establishment with an unbeatable location in the heart

reviews.title: A La Carte in Hotel Form
reviews.text:
Location is great; Red Line subway entrance is basically right outside the hotel. Staff was friendly and professional. The bedroom was clean, and there were multiple plug in options for charging devices. 

I knew ahead of time there would be a $25 per night resort fee.... and assumed there might be an accidents/incidental deposit - I was not expecting it to be $150. That was a bit upsurd. The hotel really does operate as an "a la carte" style, there is allegedly a pool in the spa; you have to pay for that, if you need better wifi than to "check emails" you'll pay additionally for that. Breakfast is *not* included (and seems to be a pretty absurd price)... technically, that $150 deposit can go towards all that stuff, you'll just get less that amount of whatever you end up using, if you do.  
I appreciate that upon check in, guests are provided a bottle of water, I think that was pretty nice, and although some reviews I read before my stay said there wouldn't be a mini fridge in the room, I was happy to find that there was indeed a usable mini fridge in my room... but there was also one full of  drinks/alcohol as well as a snack platter on top with a note that said you'd be charged for moving anything on the tray as there are sensors under each thing... I didn't appreciate that - that's a cheap gimmick that has zero business being in a hotel room. 

Overall, the location was great, and the hotel/room was in good shape.... but I definitely wouldn't pay full price to stay at the hotel. I've travelled through a handful of countries, stayed at many hotels, and I can genuinely say that if "Tourist Trap" was a hotel, it'd be this one. 

Again, overall: great location and is an interesting place with clean rooms and professional staff, but not a place I'd pay full price to stay in (especially with its a la carte style for any and every service/amenities).

reviews.title: 今回もパーマハウスヒルトンに宿泊しした
reviews.text:
今回のシカゴ滞在もパーマハウスヒルトンを利用させて頂きました。華麗で、かつ荘厳なロビーに代表されるように歴史を感じるホテルです。今回はキングスベッドルームに 3泊宿泊しましたが、長時間の空旅の疲れも初日は癒してくれましたが、2日目からはシカゴ大学の行事で隣室も含めてホテル館内は学生だらけ。大学のイベント行事終了の夜11時から深夜2時まで、部屋をはしごする学生の話し声とはしゃぐ声、ひいては、張り裂けんばかりの大合唱（隣室）。学生に対する大学側の指導もあって欲しいと思いますが、ホテル側も一般客と学生の宿泊階、エリアを分けるとか部屋の割り振りに配慮があっても良いのではないかと（息子も大学生なので同じように迷惑をかけているものと、騒ぐさまに文句が言えなかった）
あと建具が老朽化があるのか、他の客室も扉の開閉や廊下の音に対する防音は改善の余地ありでしょうか。
reviews.positives: 空港からのアクセス、ゆったりした客室、ロビーの荘厳さ

reviews.title: Severe Hygiene Concerns and Disappointing Room Reassignment at Palmer House Hilton
reviews.text:
My recent stay at Palmer House, a Hilton Hotel, was deeply disappointing and fell far below the standards I have come to expect from a property of this reputation.

During my stay, I encountered a serious hygiene issue: a cockroach was visibly crawling across the desk in my room before descending to the floor and continuing to move about. This is entirely unacceptable and raises significant concerns about the hotel’s cleanliness and pest control.

I requested a room change and was reassigned; however, the new room was noticeably smaller and located much farther from the elevator, making it considerably less convenient. When I asked for a more suitable alternative, I was offered a similar option, leaving the situation unresolved.

This experience was particularly disheartening as I had traveled to Chicago for a special occasion, expecting a comfortable and pleasant stay. Instead, it left me with a strong sense of disappointment.

Having stayed in many hotels over the years, I can say this was one of the most unsatisfactory experiences I have had. I hope the management takes prompt action to improve both hygiene standards and overall guest experience.

We stayed here 2 years ago, big difference this time. We had to pay $40 just to have a microwave in our room. They did bring it up after 1st request....but still called us 4x and texted 2x to make sure we got it.......Kicker....they still brought up a 2nd one. My husband had to argue with that gentleman that we indeed did get one already. Had to ask 3x for t.p. only 1/2 roll in br when we got here. No one knows what the other person is doing. Husband was down waiting for a delivery we placed with CVS. He didn't have his phone, but  I needed to tell him the delivery driver had car issues. I called guest services, only button marked on the phone for guest services, and the guy who answered informed me they couldn't go tell my husband to come back to room, who was just across the front desk where deliveries are left, cuz they don't do that. I'd have to walk down there. (We were on 16th floor and I have a very bad knee) So I did. The temperature in the room..... We froze cuz at 69 it was freezing at 70 we were sweating bullets!! Tablets come in room, useless.... 
So, no microwave, no coffee pot, no toilet  paper in rooms. Had a fridge, with no shelves. Just very very unhappy. The Hampton Inn a couple blocks away would have been much much cheaper!!!!  I don't recommend Palmer House. We did order breakfast the first morning to be brought up to the room. It arrived nice and hot, what little portions you got. It was good, but EXPENSIVE!!!!!   NOT A RECOMMENDED PLACE TO GO!!!!

reviews.title: Great ambience and location but service is questionable
reviews.text: I wanted to share my thoughts about this hotel. While the ambiance, lobby, and location are indeed impressive and ideal for those visiting Chicago, I can't overlook the disappointing service we encountered. The rooms had a dim atmosphere, and surprisingly, there was no body wash available in the washroom. Our conversation with the lady at the lobby, presumably the manager, left us upset as she seemed unable to assist or make us feel welcome. Considering the substantial amount of money spent, the service provided simply didn't match up. I regret to say that this was a 2-star hotel experience for us.
